title: Unclear which network is used to publish port when a container has multiple networks specified
body:
### Issue Description

While I am running a fairly outdated version due to using package manager version, I consider this more as a documentation issue, hence this post.
I am using multiple network on my containers, one for internet access, and one for cross-container communication.
However, it seems like port is forwarded through cross-container communication, and it is promptly blocked by the firewall.
I am unsure if this is a bug in old version, or intended behavior that is not well-documented (I could not find the relevant documentations).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Setup quadlet files with multiple networks. For mine, I have
```
# forgejo.container
[Container]
ContainerName=forgejo
Image=codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:10
Network=bridge
Network=forgejo.network
PublishPort=222:22
Volume=forgejo-data:/data
# Specify size as estimation fails
UserNS=auto:size=1024

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

```
# forgejo-data.volume
[Volume]
VolumeName=forgejo-data
```

```
# forgejo.network
[Network]
NetworkName=forgejo
```

2. Take iptables forward rules to only allow podman0 (the bridge network), and log the rejection
```
Chain FORWARD (policy ACCEPT 0 packets, 0 bytes)
 pkts bytes target     prot opt in     out     source               destination
14032 1511K NETAVARK_FORWARD  all  --  any    any     anywhere             anywhere             /* netavark firewall plugin rules */
   10   480 ACCEPT     tcp  --  any    podman0  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:http
 1915 99956 ACCEPT     tcp  --  any    podman0  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:https
    0     0 ACCEPT     tcp  --  any    podman0  anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:ssh
  361 19032 LOG        all  --  any    any     anywhere             anywhere             LOG level info prefix "FORWARD:REJECT:"
  361 19032 REJECT     all  --  any    any     anywhere             anywhere             reject-with icmp-host-prohibited
```



### Describe the results you received

Podman forwards through podman0 interface (the default bridge network)

### Describe the results you expected

Podman forwards through podman3 interface (the cross-container network), promptly being blocked by firewall.
```
2025-04-12T17:53:12.614037+00:00 abastro-personal kernel: FORWARD:REJECT:IN=ens3 OUT=podman3 MAC=02:00:17:00:31:7f:00:00:17:71:a5:dd:08:00 SRC=<my ip> DST=10.89.1.12 LEN=60 TOS=0x08 PREC=0x40 TTL=53 ID=5517 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=41860 DPT=22 WINDOW=64240 RES=0x00 SYN URGP=0
```

podman (default) network:
```

               }
          ],

          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": false,
          "ipam_options": {

          }
     }
]
```

forgejo network:
```
[
     {
          "name": "forgejo",
          "id": "fc7c4a62616ed86ae0babfe5fece513cde19361b2eaea23377df9e36af411047",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman3",
          "created": "2025-03-29T08:11:55.510124633Z",
          "subnets": [
               {

                    "gateway": "10.89.1.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
That is the expected behavior, if you publish ports they are always be forwarded on all networks.  The ordering is not deterministic (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12850). 
I guess in your case you want to influence the outgoing routing decision so you can also use the --opt metric=<num> to give each network a different value. By default all networks have a default route with the same metric and then the kernel round-robin between them (https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/377).

If you have a second just inter communication network you should likely just make it internal then (--internal) which would not forward any ports.

> That is the expected behavior, if you publish ports they are always be forwarded on all networks.

@Luap99 where is this behavior documented? Also, for outbound connections, does podman try all networks?
